figures.caption: Figure 4: Gate comparison under λIC = 50 (mean ± 95% CI). Adaptive models use 20 seeds for each k ∈ {20, 30, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100, 110, 120, 130} and 10 seeds elsewhere.
Figure 3: Gate comparison under λIC = 0 (mean ± 95% CI). Adaptive models use 20 seeds at k = 20 and k = 60 (used for statistical testing) and 10 seeds elsewhere.

Table 1: Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank tests across seeds comparing IC gates for the adaptive model. Lower is better for both metrics. Winner indicates whether A or B attains lower error. We report both raw two-sided p-values and Holm-adjusted two-sided p-values; Holm correction is applied across the tested (k,metric) pairs within each setting.
Figure 2: Full-scale ReL2E versus stiffness for λIC = 50.
Figure 1: Gate comparison under λIC = 50 (mean ± 95% CI). Adaptive models use 20 seeds for each k ∈ {20, 30, 50, 60} and 10 seeds elsewhere.
